title: Melty Mexican Baby Pak Koi Tacos
description: A peppery and gooey Mexican dish featuring baby pak koi, long fermentation dough, and a satvik friendly approach, all grilled to perfection on a binchotan grill.
instructions: Preheat the binchotan grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Divide the long fermentation dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of olive oil on each dough circle and spread evenly.
Add a few pieces of baby pak koi to the center of each dough circle.
Sprinkle shredded pepper jack cheese, diced red onion, and chopped cilantro over the pak koi.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Fold the dough in half to enclose the filling and press the edges together to seal.
Place the tacos on the preheated binchotan grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until the dough is crispy and the cheese is melted.
Serve immediately, with a squeeze of lime juice over the top.
